name: KB Plan
type: directive
category: knowledge-management
scope: project
trigger: /kb-plan
version: 1.2.11
---

# /kb-plan — Analyze and write the next KB action plan

Your task: produce or refine a runtime plan describing which KB actions the user needs next, from the surface defined by the four CLI lifecycle commands and any custom KB work.

You are operating under the master `@kb` agent contract at `.github/agents/kb.agent.md`. Honor its read order, governance, and citation rules.

## Inputs to inspect

Always read (silent, no narration):

0. **CLI status** — run `kb status --json` (or fall back to `npx -y @williamduong/kb@latest status --json` if `kb` is not on PATH). The CLI is the single source of truth for `presence` (`fresh` / `healthy` / `partial`) and for the resolved `state.json` path. Do NOT use file_search or filesystem probes alone — most IDEs exclude `.git/` from search by default and will misclassify `private-git` installs as `partial`.
1. `state.json` — the CLI's `--json` output already includes the parsed state object (`schemaVersion`, `cliVersion`, `templateVersion`, `metadataPolicy`, `ideIntegration`, `storageMode`, etc.). If `presence === 'fresh'`, treat as `not-initialized`. If `presence === 'partial'`, abstain and ask the user to follow the recovery steps printed by `kb status`.
2. `knowledge-base/00-start-here/repository-revision-state.md` (if present) — drift baseline. In `private-git` mode this is at `.git/project-kb/content/00-start-here/repository-revision-state.md`; consult `state.contentRoot` from CLI output.
3. `knowledge-base/00-start-here/finalization-plan.md` (if present) — outstanding finalization items.
4. Workspace `package.json` (if present) — for `projectName` detection.
5. Latest published CLI version via `npm view @williamduong/kb version` (only if `state.cliVersion` looks behind; otherwise skip to save tokens).
6. Existing plan file at `knowledge-base/.kb/runtime-plan.md` (if present). In `private-git` mode the plan still lives under the visible `knowledge-base/` mount (which is a junction/symlink to `.git/project-kb/content/`).

## Decision rules

Map observations to recommended actions. Each action MUST be one of:

- `init` — KB not installed in workspace
- `update` — `state.cliVersion` < latest published OR `state.templateVersion` < bundled template
- `maintain` — drift detected OR working tree dirty OR `kb_state` queue has unresolved `needs-review`
- `uninstall` — user explicitly requests teardown (only when user says so in the chat)
- `custom:<short-id>` — domain-specific KB work the user mentioned (e.g. `custom:fill-frontend-docs`)

Default ordering when multiple apply: `init → update → maintain → custom → uninstall`.

## Plan file format

Write to `knowledge-base/.kb/runtime-plan.md` (create parent directories if needed). Use this exact shape:

```markdown
---
plan_version: 1
created: <ISO date>
last_updated: <ISO date>
current_step: 1
metadata_policy: <advisory|strict>
---

# KB Runtime Plan

> Generated by /kb-plan. Edit by hand or refine via /kb-plan with extra instructions.

## Steps

- [ ] step-1 (status: pending) — <action> — <one-line rationale>
- [ ] step-2 (status: pending) — <action> — <one-line rationale>

## Notes

- <free-form notes the user added>
```

Rules:

- One step per recommended action. Do not bundle.
- `current_step` points to the next `pending` step (1-based).
- When user invokes `/kb-plan` again with extra text after the slash command, treat the extra text as refinement: keep `done` steps untouched, regenerate or reorder `pending` steps according to user intent, append any user notes to the `## Notes` section.
- Never delete `done` steps. Mark obsolete pending steps as `(status: skipped)` and add the reason inline.
- If KB is `not-initialized`, the first pending step MUST be `init` and `metadata_policy` defaults to `advisory`.

## Output to chat

After writing the plan, print a concise summary to chat. The final block MUST end with an explicit "What to do next" menu so the user knows whether to converse or run a command:

```
KB plan ready: knowledge-base/.kb/runtime-plan.md
- current_step: <n>
- pending: <count>
- next action: <action of current_step> — <one-line rationale>

What to do next (pick one):
  1. Run `/kb-run` to execute step <n> now.
  2. Reply `/kb-plan <change>` to refine the plan (e.g. reorder, drop, add steps).
  3. Reply with a question or task (`@kb …` or just plain chat) to keep talking; the plan stays as-is.
```

Do not run any step. /kb-plan only plans.

## Refinement loop

If the user sends `/kb-plan <extra text>`:
1. Read existing plan.
2. Apply the user's intent (add/remove/reorder pending steps, edit notes).
3. Re-write the plan file with updated `last_updated` and (if needed) `current_step`.
4. Print updated summary.

## Boundaries

- Do not call CLI commands. /kb-run does that.
- Do not write outside `knowledge-base/.kb/runtime-plan.md` and (when needed) parent directories.
- If `state.json` exists but is malformed, abstain and ask the user to run `kb doctor`.
